gusub.__tinydesc: Get a new UTF8 string after a global pattern substitution

ufind.__tinydesc: Search for the first pattern in an UFT8 string

split.__description: Splits a string into substrings by a separator.
split.__example:
-- Lua string.split example
local console = require "console"

local str = "Hello[space]World[space]by[space]LuaRT[space]!"

for word in each(str:split("[space]")) do
    console.write(word.." ")
end

__params.__description: The string that will be divided into substrings.

A string that will be used as a separator to divide the string into substrings.

This function can split ASCII or UTF8 encoded strings.

split.__tinydesc: Split an UTF8 string into substrings
split.__returns: Returns a list of substrings as a table.

dump.__tinydesc: Get the bytecode of a function

upper.__description: Converts all lowercase characters in a string into uppercase characters.
upper.__example:
local console = require "console"

print('Enter your name : ')
local name = console.readln();

if name == name:upper() then
	print("Please don't shout !")
else
	print('Hi '..name..' !')
end

__params.__description: The string to be used to produce a string with uppercase characters.

upper.__tinydesc: Convert an UTF8 string to uppercase
upper.__returns: Returns a new string with the same content but uppercase characters.

capitalize.__description: Capitalize the string : All letters are lowered except the first character of the string.
capitalize.__example:
local console = require "console"

console.write('Enter your name : ')
local name = string.capitalize(console.readln())
print('Hi '..name..' !')

__params.__description:
A string that will be capitalized.

This function will try to capitalize the string, even for UTF8 characters.

capitalize.__returns: Returns a new string with the same content but capizalized.

gsub.__tinydesc: Get a new string after a global pattern substitution

umatch.__tinydesc: Get an UTF8 string match from a pattern

usub.__tinydesc: Get a substring in a UTF8 string

ureverse.__tinydesc: Get a new reversed UTF8 string

gumatch.__tinydesc: Get an UTF8 pattern matching iterator

__description: The string module provides powerful functions for string manipulation

similarity.__description: Calculate the similarity between two strings, using Levenshtein distance algorithm.
similarity.__example:
local word = "bird"

-- outputs 60
print("Similarity between 'bird' and 'bread' : "..word:similarity('bread'))

__params.__description: The first string to be evaluated for similiarity.
The second string to be evaluated for similiarity with the previous one.

similarity.__tinydesc: Compute UTF8 string similarity using Levenshtein distance algorithm
similarity.__returns: Returns a number between 0 (no similarity at all) and 100 (strings are the same)

__tinydesc: Enhances Lua’s string manipulation with additional utilities and UTF8 support

normalize.__description: Normalize the string : All accentued characters are replaced by non accentued ones.
normalize.__example:
local summer_in_french = "été"

-- prints the string, but normalized
-- should output "ete"
print(summer_in_french:normalize())
normalize.__returns: Returns a new string with the same content but normalized.

string length operator.__description:
As stated in the Lua 5.4 programming manual (see The Length Operator), the returned length of a string is its number of bytes. This behaviour is respected in Luart to preserve compatibility with standard Lua with ASCII characters.

To get the length in characters for UTF8 strings, do not use the # operator, use string.ulen() instead.
string length operator.__tinydesc: Get the size of the string in bytes
string length operator.__example:
-- declare an UTF8 string
local summer_in_french = "�t�"

-- outputs 5 (in bytes)
print( #summer_in_french )

-- outputs 3 (in characters) 
print( summer_in_french:ulen() )

string iteration.__description: UTF8 strings in Luart are iterable through the Luart helper function each(), returning the next character in the string with each loop iteration. Each iteration returns the character as a string.
string iteration.__tinydesc: Iterate over an UTF8 string, character by character
string iteration.__example:
local console = require "console"

console.write("\nEnter your name : ")
local name = console.readln()
console.write("\nLet me spell it... ")

for char in each(name) do
	console.write("["..char.."] ")
end
console.write("\n")

lower.__description: Converts all uppercase characters in a string into lowercase characters.
lower.__example:
local console = require "console"

console.write('Enter a word : ')
local word = console.readln():lower()

print('Here the word in lower characters : '..word)

__params.__description:
The string to be used to produce a string with lowercase characters.

This function will try to convert the string, even for UTF8 characters.

lower.__tinydesc: Convert an UTF8 string to lowercase
lower.__returns: Returns a new string with the same content but lowercase characters.

byte.__tinydesc: Gets the bytes of a string

usearch.__description: Search an UTF8 string for the first occurence of a substring, using plain text (no patterns). Faster than string.ufind()
usearch.__example:
local text = "The sun will shine again !"

-- outputs 9 and 12
print(text:usearch("will"))

__params.__description: An optional number indicating where to start the search. Its default value is 1 and canbe negative.

usearch.__tinydesc: Fast UTF8 string search without patterns
usearch.__returns: If the function finds a match, it returns two number : the character position where this occurrence starts and ends. If the sub-string has not been found, it returns nil.

unpack.__tinydesc: Get values from a serialized string

len.__tinydesc: Get the string length in bytes

packsize.__tinydesc: Get the size of a serialized string

find.__tinydesc: Search for the first pattern in a string

ulen.__description: Gets the length in characters of the provided UTF8 string.
ulen.__example:
-- declare an UTF8 string (file must be saved with UTF8 encoding)
local summer_in_french = "été"

-- outputs 5 (in bytes)
print( #summer_in_french )

-- outputs 5 (in bytes)
print( string.len(summer_in_french) )

-- outputs 3 (in characters) 
print( summer_in_french:ulen() )

__params.__description: The UTF8 string whose length will be returned

ulen.__tinydesc: Get an UTF8 string length in characters

char.__tinydesc: Get a string from successive bytes

rep.__tinydesc: Concatenate an UTF8 string multiple times

match.__tinydesc: Get a match from a pattern

uchar.__tinydesc: Get an UTF8 string from successive bytes

ubyte.__tinydesc: Gets the bytes of an UTF8 string

format.__tinydesc: Format an UTF8 string with multiple values
